examples: There is no evidence for such a historical personage.
Galileo is the only contemporary personage, the only seventeenth-century figure, even so much as mentioned in Paradise Lost.
In fact, a new personage had entered on the scene.
He was a gentleman, a great personage and an enormous loss to Great Britain when he was killed.
In his place lived James Lawrence Fahey, a respected personage who had chaired a curling club and even been mayor of a small town.
